steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes the sentence "many of whom, like neuroscience and biophysics expert Elba Serrano, ______ this mechanism." The subject is "many" — plural. The phrase "like Elba Serrano" is a parenthetical example inserted between the subject and the verb; it does not change the subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘study’: Base-form verb used with plural subjects (they study). Agrees with "Many." ✓
  • Choice B — ‘has studied’: Singular present-perfect (he/she has studied). Disagrees with the plural subject "Many." ✗
  • Choice C — ‘studies’: Third-person singular present tense (he/she studies). Disagrees with the plural subject "Many." ✗
  • Choice D — ‘is studying’: Third-person singular progressive. Disagrees with the plural subject "Many." ✗
That confirms A is correct.
